prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde (Boletim Epidemiológico de Tuberculose 2024) / SINAN
prevalence.trend: stable to slightly decreasing overall; persistent hotspots in large cities and prisons

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Sputum smear and/or rapid molecular test (TRM-TB/Xpert), culture when available; chest X-ray supportive; notification per Ministério da Saúde guidelines

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: targeted screening of respiratory symptomatic patients in APS; contact tracing coverage variable by municipality
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~70% treatment success; lower in TB-HIV and social vulnerability contexts

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: TRM-TB prioritized in SUS; smear microscopy and radiography widely used; culture/ DST concentrated in reference labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SUS standard regimen for drug-susceptible TB (rifampicin/isoniazid/pyrazinamide/ethambutol) with directly observed therapy when indicated; MDR-TB managed in reference centers
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Social determinants (overcrowding, homelessness), high prison burden, delayed diagnosis in primary care, and MDR-TB management capacity outside capitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal access to diagnosis and treatment via SUS, compulsory notification and surveillance, and national TB control program infrastructure
